or Diminutive month. One such month had occurred in the Saka
year 1744 and the next was in 1885 (Saka year).1]
He should also be well-versed in the calculation of the begin-
ning and ending times of the cycle of sixty years, a yuga (a five-
year period), a year, a month, a day, a Hora (hour), as well as
of their respective lords.

10 Bfhat SamhitZ
[The names of the five years constituting a Yuga and their
lords as well as those of the 12 Yugas are explained by the author
in Chapter VIII—23 and 24 infra. For Hora see XIII
—42.]
He should also be capable of explaining by means of argu-
ments the similarities and dissimilarities as well as the appropria-
teness or otherwise of the different systems of measurement of time
headed by the solar system.
[The differences among the four systems are to be found out
by calculations, while the propriety or otherwise of a particular
